purpose: Defines the required structure and fail-closed validation rules for a GovernanceMutationProof — the artifact that binds /execute -> /proof for governance mutations, closing the gap between Governance Mutation Authorization (GMA) and runtime proof persistence.
canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.
closure_condition: Specified -> Authorized -> Compiled -> Executed -> Proven is enforced end-to-end for governance mutations. A governance mutation that completes execution must produce a lineage-bound proof or collapse to NULL.
emission_surface: POST /proof — additive, optional. Engages only when the request body includes a governance_mutation_authorization object carrying GMA lineage fields (gma_id, status, decision_id, continuity_id, session_id). Non-governance-mutation /proof calls are unaffected.
emission_timing: Occurs only after the underlying execution proof has passed every existing /proof eligibility check (execution lineage, session/continuity/authority binding, freshness, hash equality, attestation, replay ambiguity) and the proof_registry row plus authority consumption have been committed.

lineage_binding.session_id: must equal the executing session_id bound to the underlying execution and authority
lineage_binding.continuity_id: must equal execution.continuity_id (the continuity lineage the execution was bound to)
lineage_binding.decision_id: must equal the executing decision_id and the GMA's declared decision_id
lineage_binding.authority_id: must equal authority_registry.authority_id consumed by the underlying proof
lineage_binding.gma_id: identifier of the Governance Mutation Authorization that authorized this mutation
lineage_binding.compiled_object_hash: validated_object_hash of the COMPILED canonical AEO (the compiled object hash)
lineage_binding.executed_object_hash: validated_object_hash recorded on the EXECUTED execution_registry row (the executed object hash)

lineage_hash_input: canonicalize({ object_type: 'GovernanceMutationProofLineage', session_id, continuity_id, decision_id, authority_id, gma_id, compiled_object_hash, executed_object_hash }), then sha256 of the canonical JSON
proof_id_derivation: GMP-<lineage_hash[0:32]> — deterministic; identical lineage always yields the identical proof_id, which is the basis for replay detection

fail_closed_rules.condition: any required lineage or hash field is absent or empty
compiled_object_hash != executed_object_hash — the compiled object and the executed object diverged
the GMA's declared decision_id, continuity_id, or session_id does not match the executing lineage
the deterministic lineage_hash / proof_id has already been persisted to governance_mutation_proof_registry

binding_triggers: trg_governance_mutation_proof_requires_valid_proof — aborts insert unless a matching EXECUTED-lineage row exists in proof_registry for execution_proof_id/decision_id/executed_object_hash

implementation_path: src/lib/governance-mutation-proof.ts (pure, fail-closed builder); wired additively into the POST /proof handler in src/index.ts after authority consumption succeeds
